content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA containing NNN elements. Count the number of positive integers XXX satisfying the following condition :


(X⊕Ai)<Ai(X \oplus A_i) < A_i(X⊕Ai​)<Ai​ for all 1≤i≤N1 \le i \le N1≤i≤N, where ⊕\oplus⊕ represents the Bitwise XOR operator.
It can be proven that the answer is always finite under the given constraints.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line contains NNN - the size of the array
The second line contains NNN integers - A1,A2,...,ANA_1, A_2, ..., A_NA1​,A2​,...,AN​.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the number of valid positive integers XXX.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test 1 : The only number satisfying the constraint is 111.

1⊕1=0<11 \oplus 1 = 0 < 11⊕1=0<1
1⊕3=2<31 \oplus 3 = 2 < 31⊕3=2<3

Test Case 2 : It can be proven that there is no valid number.
